validation_cycles.overview: Before a PRD can proceed to user story decomposition, it must pass validation across all 8 dimensions. Each dimension receives a confidence score: low, medium, or high. ALL 8 dimensions must reach "high" confidence before proceeding. This gate is not advisory — it is blocking.

dimension.name: structure_completeness
dimension.description: All 14 required PRD sections are present and contain substantive content.

scoring.low: Sections are missing, or contain placeholder text (TBD, TODO, see later).
scoring.medium: All sections present but some lack depth or specificity.
scoring.high: All 14 sections present with substantive, specific content — no placeholders.

probing_questions.question: Which sections still contain placeholder or generic content?
Could a new team member understand each section without asking clarifying questions?

dimension.name: testability
dimension.description: Every acceptance criterion maps to a specific, automatable test condition.

scoring.low: Criteria use subjective or unmeasurable terms: "intuitive", "fast", "user-friendly", "clean".
scoring.medium: Most criteria are testable but one or more remain vague or threshold-less.
scoring.high: Every criterion has an explicit, measurable threshold and an observable pass/fail condition.

probing_questions.question: For each criterion, can you write a test assertion right now? What is the exact condition?
What is the numeric threshold for performance criteria (load time, throughput, error rate)?
What defines "correct" output for LLM-generated or AI-powered features?

dimension.name: consistency
dimension.description: No contradictions exist between sections; tensions are explicitly acknowledged and resolved.

scoring.low: Direct contradictions exist between sections (e.g., offline-first AND real-time sync required).
scoring.medium: Potential tensions exist that are not explicitly resolved.
scoring.high: All sections align; any tensions are acknowledged with an explicit resolution rationale.

probing_questions.question: Does [requirement in section X] conflict with [requirement in section Y]? Which takes priority?
Is [term] defined consistently across all sections?

dimension.name: security_privacy
dimension.description: Security and privacy considerations are addressed adequately for the product type and data handled.

scoring.low: No security or privacy section; sensitive data handling is undescribed.
scoring.medium: Basic security mentions exist but key concerns (credential storage, PII handling, threat model) are unaddressed.
scoring.high: Sensitive data identified and handling strategy described; credential security addressed; privacy obligations acknowledged; key threats mitigated.

probing_questions.question: Where is [sensitive data / API key / PII] stored? What is the encryption strategy?
What is the threat model? What happens if an attacker gains access to [credential/token]?
Which data qualifies as PII under applicable regulations? How is it retained and deleted?

dimension.name: clarity_precision
dimension.description: No subjective or ambiguous terms are used without an explicit definition or measurable proxy.

scoring.low: Multiple undefined subjective terms: "mature aesthetic", "feels fast", "user-friendly", "relevant results".
scoring.medium: Most terms defined but some subjective language remains without measurable proxy.
scoring.high: All terms are either objectively defined or have an explicit measurable proxy agreed by the user.

probing_questions.question: What does "[subjective term]" mean specifically? Can you point to a reference product or give a measurable threshold?
How would two engineers independently interpret "[ambiguous phrase]"? Would they agree?

dimension.name: technical_feasibility
dimension.description: Technology choices are viable for the stated requirements and justified with known trade-offs.

scoring.low: Technology choices are assumed without justification; known incompatibilities or risks are unaddressed.
scoring.medium: Choices are plausible but key trade-offs or compatibility risks are not documented.
scoring.high: Each significant technology choice is justified, key trade-offs are acknowledged, and known risks are mitigated.

probing_questions.question: What evidence supports that [technology] can handle [requirement] at the expected scale or context?
What are the known trade-offs of choosing [technology] over alternatives?
Is there a known incompatibility between [technology A] and [technology B] in this context?

dimension.research_dispatch_trigger: When the planner cannot confidently assess technical feasibility, REQUIRE dispatching an sdlc-project-research agent before scoring this dimension.

dimension.name: scope_definition
dimension.description: In-scope and out-of-scope are explicit, exhaustive, and leave no gray areas.

scoring.low: Scope is implied or vague; no explicit out-of-scope list; boundary cases unaddressed.
scoring.medium: Core scope is defined but some boundary cases or edge behaviors are unaddressed.
scoring.high: In-scope and out-of-scope are exhaustive; boundary behaviors are explicitly defined; no gray areas remain.

probing_questions.question: What happens when a user tries to do [out-of-scope item]? Is the behavior defined?
Are there any features that are "sort of in scope" but not explicitly called out?
What is the expected behavior at the boundary between [feature A] and [feature B]?

dimension.name: downstream_readiness
dimension.description: Designers and developers have enough detail to begin work without requiring additional clarification sessions.

scoring.low: Key design or implementation decisions are deferred; navigation, data model, or UI flow is undefined.
scoring.medium: Most decisions are present but some key details (screen flow, data shape, API contract) are missing.
scoring.high: All decisions needed to begin design and implementation are present; no blocking ambiguities remain.

probing_questions.question: What is the default screen when the app opens? Is the navigation flow defined?
What are the exact fields in the data model for [entity]?
Is there an API contract or data format defined for [integration point]?

validation_process.step: After a PRD draft is created or significantly updated, run self-assessment across all 8 dimensions.
Present the scorecard table to the user, explicitly naming the key issue for each non-high dimension.
For each low or medium dimension, present the targeted probing questions from that dimension's question bank.
User provides answers. Update the PRD and re-score the affected dimensions.
GATE: Do not proceed to user story decomposition until all 8 are "high".
If the user explicitly overrides the gate, require written acknowledgment of risk per each non-high dimension before proceeding.

validation_cycles.scorecard_format:
Present as a markdown table after each validation run:
| # | Dimension | Confidence | Key Issue | |---|-----------|-----------|-----------| | 1 | Structure and Completeness | high/medium/low | [specific issue or "none"] | | 2 | Testability | high/medium/low | [specific issue or "none"] | | 3 | Consistency | high/medium/low | [specific issue or "none"] | | 4 | Security and Privacy | high/medium/low | [specific issue or "none"] | | 5 | Clarity and Precision | high/medium/low | [specific issue or "none"] | | 6 | Technical Feasibility | high/medium/low | [specific issue or "none"] | | 7 | Scope Definition | high/medium/low | [specific issue or "none"] | | 8 | Downstream Readiness | high/medium/low | [specific issue or "none"] |
Follow with the targeted questions for each non-high dimension, grouped by dimension.
